title: Forget Nvidia. These 5 S&P 500 stocks are quietly going all in on AI.
excerpt: The “Mag Seven” has become the “Bag Seven.” Sharp tech investors are looking elsewhere.
content_html:
The "Mag Seven" stocks have become the "Bag Seven" — or worse. That was fun while it lasted.

Through the first half of 2026, the group of megacap tech stocks known as the Magnificent Seven (eight if you count SpaceX SPCX) were down from their highs. Meanwhile, the stalwart S&P 500 SPX was up more than 9%. For the AI hyperscalers, "magnificent" has become mediocre and the trade has lost momentum.

Most Read from MarketWatch

  • I will definitely claim Social Security early. Why do so few people talk about the elephant in the room?

  • The co-founder of Stocktwits dumped chip stocks before their 20% slide. Where he's putting his money now.

  • Do I use $300,000 of my $1.2 million retirement savings so my daughter can attend her dream college?

It's not hard to see why. Most of the AI-centered businesses are overpriced (roughly 7x revenue vs 2.7x for the S&P 500 SPX). Many have engaged in roundabout financing deals — Wall Street's version of check kiting.

Since the obvious artificial-intelligence plays are overbought, investors have been left to look for alternatives. Those could be under-the-radar AI companies (already largely discovered), international stocks (currency risk with a strong U.S. dollar DXY), financials (credit risk) or defense (geopolitical risk).

For investors who believe in AI as transformational for business, there is one truly overlooked option: existing S&P 500 companies that have gone all in on AI.

Since February I've been researching more than 130 companies to gauge their adoption of AI, their investments and their business results — including whether they've been able to eliminate what's often their biggest cost center: workers. The companies in this index were chosen deliberately — established large-, mid-, and small-cap businesses across 14 industries that use or could use AI, and not the Nvidias NVDA and Anthropics that sell it. The question wasn't who's building AI. It was who's actually deploying it.

What I found for the vast majority of these companies is that in the three years since the launch of ChatGPT, most have talked a lot about AI, but just a handful have produced an impact that lands on the financial statement or regulatory disclosures. Most companies aren't cutting jobs (though that number is growing). Most aren't doing meaningful spending on AI. Most don't have anything like a new, game-changing innovative tool (that works).

That said, some companies are genuinely using AI. For instance, Costco COST doesn't disclose a lot about its use of AI, but analysts say the retailer is aggressively using it for inventory, pricing and supply-chain logistics. Maybe that's true, but there's not a lot of numbers coming out of the company. How much more efficient could it be? And I don't trust Costco. You go in for a $1.50 hot dog and you walk out having spent $150.

content_html_zh:
“七大”股票已经变成了“七大”股票——或者更糟。 持续的时候很有趣。

到 2026 年上半年，被称为七巨头（如果算上 SpaceX SPCX 的话，就有八只）的巨型科技股从高点下跌。 与此同时，强劲的标准普尔 500 SPX 指数上涨超过 9%。 对于人工智能超大规模企业来说，“宏伟”已经变得平庸，行业也失去了动力。

大多数阅读来自 MarketWatch

  • 我一定会尽早申请社会保障。 为什么很少有人谈论房间里的大象？
  • Stocktwits 联合创始人在芯片股下跌 20% 之前抛售了该股。 他现在把钱放在哪里。
  • 我是否可以使用 120 万美元退休储蓄中的 30 万美元让我的女儿能够进入她梦想的大学？

不难看出原因。 大多数以人工智能为中心的企业定价过高（大约是收入的 7 倍，而 S&P 500 SPX 的收入是 2.7 倍）。 许多人都参与了迂回融资交易——华尔街版的支票风筝。

由于明显的人工智能股票已经超买，投资者只能寻找替代品。 这些可能是不为人所知的人工智能公司（已经大部分被发现）、国际股票（美元 DXY 走强的货币风险）、金融（信用风险）或国防（地缘政治风险）。

对于相信人工智能能够改变商业的投资者来说，有一个真正被忽视的选择：现有的标准普尔 500 强公司已经全力投资人工智能。

自 2 月份以来，我一直在研究 130 多家公司，以衡量他们对人工智能的采用、投资和业务成果，包括他们是否能够消除通常最大的成本中心：工人。 该指数中的公司是经过精心挑选的——在 14 个行业中使用或可能使用人工智能的大、中、小型企业，而不是出售人工智能的 Nvidia NVDA 和 Anthropics。 问题不是谁在构建人工智能。 是谁真正部署了它。

我对这些公司中的绝大多数发现，自ChatGPT推出以来的三年里，大多数公司都在谈论人工智能，但只有少数公司对财务报表或监管披露产生了影响。 大多数公司都没有裁员（尽管这个数字正在增长）。 大多数人没有在人工智能上进行有意义的支出。 大多数人没有任何新的、改变游戏规则的创新工具（有效）。

也就是说，一些公司正在真正使用人工智能。 例如，Costco COST 并未透露太多有关人工智能的使用情况，但分析师表示，该零售商正在积极将其用于库存、定价和供应链物流。 也许这是真的，但公司并没有透露太多数据。 效率能提高多少？ 而且我不信任好市多。 你进去买了一个 1.50 美元的热狗，然后你就花了 150 美元走出去。
